name: Asbestos-Cement Roofing Sheets
description: Corrugated or flat cement sheets containing 10-15% asbestos fibers. Very common in developing countries. Gray, rigid sheets often used for roofing and wall cladding.
recommendation_intact: Monitor condition annually. Do not power-wash or drill.
recommendation_damaged: Do not touch. Contact a certified asbestos removal company.
recommendation_renovation: Professional asbestos inspection required before ANY work on or near these materials.

name: Vinyl-Asbestos Floor Tiles (9×9 inch)
description: 9×9 inch floor tiles are a strong indicator of asbestos content. Common in homes, schools, and offices built between 1950-1980. Often dark-colored with a slightly waxy feel.
recommendation_intact: Safe if undamaged. Do not sand, scrape, or remove yourself.
recommendation_damaged: Avoid disturbing. Cover with new flooring if possible. Consult professional for removal.
recommendation_renovation: Professional inspection required. Do NOT remove tiles yourself — the adhesive (mastic) may also contain asbestos.

name: Pipe and Boiler Insulation
description: White or gray chalky insulation wrapped around pipes, boilers, and ducts. Often found in basements and utility rooms. Extremely dangerous when deteriorating — fibers become airborne easily.
recommendation_intact: Have a professional assess condition. Do not touch or disturb.
recommendation_damaged: DANGER: Do not enter area without protection. Contact certified asbestos removal immediately.
recommendation_renovation: Mandatory professional removal before any work in the area.

name: Textured Ceiling Coating (Popcorn Ceiling)
description: Sprayed-on textured ceiling coating popular from 1950s-1980s. Bumpy, cottage-cheese-like texture. May contain up to 10% asbestos. Common in North American homes.
recommendation_intact: Do not scrape, sand, or drill into ceiling. Monitor for water damage.
recommendation_damaged: Do not disturb. If water-damaged, contain area and contact professional.
recommendation_renovation: Professional testing required before any ceiling work. Never scrape or remove without certified abatement.
